zh.lifeSummary: 當Nicholas de Verdun於大約1174年出生在英國斯塔福爾斯塔福爾 波藍茲時，他的父親Bertram de Verdun III26歲，母親Rohesia de Windsor33歲。他和Clementia le Boteler至少有2個女兒。他去世於1231年10月23日在他的家鄉享年58歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Verdun
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: habitational name from any of several places in France called Verdun. The placename is probably of Gaulish origin, deriving from ver(n) ‘alder’ + dūn ‘hill, fortress’.
Spanish and Catalan: habitational name from Verdú in Catalonia (Spain), which has the same origin as the French name (see 1 above).
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Verdon  , a cognate of 1 above. This surname is very rare in Britain.

variants.name: Virden
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Verdun  .

variants.name: Verdon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Marcel, Pierre, Renaud, Sylvie.
French: habitational name from (Le) Verdon, the name of several places in various parts of France, for example in Dordogne, Gironde, and Marne. Compare   Ferdon  .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Verdun, the name of a fief in Vessey (Manche), France. Compare   Verdun  .

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Nicholas de Verdun was born about 1174, in Staffordshire Moorlands, Staffordshire, England, his father, Bertram de Verdun III, was 26 and his mother, Rohesia de Windsor, was 33. He had at least 2 daughters with Clementia le Boteler. He died on 23 October 1231, in his hometown, at the age of 58.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Nicholas de Verdun est né environ 1174, à Staffordshire Moorlands, Staffordshire, Angleterre, son père, Bertram de Verdun III, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Rohesia de Windsor, avait 33 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 filles avec Clementia le Boteler. Il est décédé le 23 octobre 1231, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 58 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Nicholas de Verdun um 1174 in Staffordshire Moorlands, Staffordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Bertram de Verdun III 26 und seine Mutter Rohesia de Windsor 33. Er hatte mit Clementia le Boteler mindestens 2 Töchter. Er starb am 23. Oktober 1231 im Alter von 58 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Nicholas de Verdun è nato il circa 1174 nel Staffordshire Moorlands, Staffordshire, England, il suo padre, Bertram de Verdun III, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Rohesia de Windsor, aveva 33 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figlie con Clementia le Boteler. Morì il 23 ottobre 1231, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 58 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Verdunが約1174年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアスタフォードシュアイア ムールランズで生まれた時，父Bertram de Verdun IIIは26歳，母Rohesia de Windsorは33歳であった。彼はClementia le Botelerとの間に少なくとも2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1231年10月23日に故郷で58歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Verdun(이)가 대략 1174년 영국 스태퍼드실르 스태퍼드실르 무를랜드즈에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Bertram de Verdun III(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Rohesia de Windsor(은)는 33세였다. 그는 Clementia le Boteler(와)과 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1231년 10월 23일 그의 고향에서 58세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Nicholas de Verdun nasceu aproximadamente 1174, em Staffordshire Moorlands, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Bertram de Verdun III, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Rohesia de Windsor, tinha 33 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhas com Clementia le Boteler. Ele faleceu em 23 de outubro de 1231, em sua cidade natal, com 58 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Nicholas de Verdun родился около 1174 г. в местности стаффордшир мурландс, стаффордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Bertram de Verdun III было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Rohesia de Windsor, 33. У него было по крайней мере 2 дочери от Clementia le Boteler. Он умер 23 октября 1231 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 58 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Nicholas de Verdun nació aproximadamente 1174, en Staffordshire Moorlands, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Bertram de Verdun III, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Rohesia de Windsor, tenía 33 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijas con Clementia le Boteler. Murió el 23 de octubre de 1231, en su pueblo, a la edad de 58 años.
